summary: Obfuscated dropper fetches and executes remote code on require()
details: @sql-trigger/nodesql ships a single heavily obfuscated index.js (obfuscator.io with a 241-entry RC4+base64 string array, control-flow flattening, hex-arithmetic constants) as its main module. On require(), the module assembles a hardcoded IPv4 destination via four String.concat calls joined by '.', performs an HTTP GET to that endpoint via axios, writes the response body to a file in os.tmpdir() with flag 'w+', and then exec's the dropped file via child_process with windowsHide:true and cwd set to the temp directory. Two process.on handlers silently swallow any errors raised by the fetch/exec chain. The package metadata advertises 'simple node for sql fetch' with keywords ['sql','fetch'], but no SQL functionality is present — the obfuscated dropper is the package's only behavior. Any developer who installs and require()s this package immediately fetches and runs attacker-controlled code from the hardcoded IP, with no opt-in, no logging, and an active effort to hide the command on Windows.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
